rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in the appropriate AADL scope: component type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings.
Use visible AADL features and declared helpers in AGREE expressions; avoid unresolved references or AADL property names as variables.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed within valid AADL component type or implementation scopes.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible features or declared helpers.
Component type contracts must not depend on implementation-only subcomponent paths.
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
Requiremenet analyst tasks and compact semantics
